title: 5 Essential Cillian Murphy Movies to Stream Now: A Guide to His Finest Performances
body:
Cillian Murphy's Star Power Soars as 'Peaky Blinders' Returns, But His Filmography is a Treasure Trove of Hidden Gems

Fans of the hit BBC series 'Peaky Blinders' are rejoicing as the show's beloved star, Cillian Murphy, reprises his iconic role as Tommy Shelby. With the show's revival generating significant buzz, many are eager to explore the talented actor's impressive filmography. While Murphy's performances on screen have captivated audiences worldwide, his movie career is a treasure trove of critically acclaimed and thought-provoking films that warrant attention.

One of Murphy's earliest and most notable roles was in the psychological thriller '28 Days Later' (2002), where he played Jim, a survivor of a deadly virus that turns people into rage-filled zombies. This film not only showcased Murphy's versatility as an actor but also marked a pivotal moment in the zombie genre. Directed by Danny Boyle, '28 Days Later' is a gripping and visually stunning film that set the tone for Murphy's future performances.

Murphy's collaborations with acclaimed director Christopher Nolan have also yielded some of his most memorable roles. In 'Batman Begins' (2005), he played Dr. Jonathan Crane, also known as the Scarecrow, a complex and terrifying villain who pushes Bruce Wayne to his limits. Nolan's reinterpretation of the Batman mythology marked a new era for the character, and Murphy's chilling portrayal of the Scarecrow remains an unforgettable aspect of the film.

Another standout performance by Murphy is in the biographical drama 'Inception' (2010), where he played Robert Fischer, the son of a wealthy businessman who becomes the target of a complex heist. As part of an ensemble cast, Murphy brought depth and nuance to his character, making him a compelling and relatable presence on screen. Nolan's ambitious sci-fi epic is a masterclass in storytelling, and Murphy's contribution is a testament to his skill as a supporting actor.

In addition to his work with Nolan, Murphy has also demonstrated his range in films like 'Breakfast on Pluto' (2005), a critically acclaimed drama that explores the life of a young trans woman in 1970s Ireland. Murphy's portrayal of Patrick/Patricia, a character struggling to find identity and acceptance, is a poignant and powerful performance that earned him widespread acclaim.

As fans eagerly await the return of 'Peaky Blinders', the spotlight shines on Cillian Murphy's impressive filmography. With a career spanning over two decades, Murphy has proven himself to be a versatile and talented actor capable of bringing complexity and depth to a wide range of roles. As his star power continues to soar, it's clear that his film career is only just beginning to scratch the surface of its potential. With a new generation of viewers discovering his work, it's an exciting time to be a fan of Cillian Murphy – and with his filmography offering a wealth of hidden gems, there's never been a better time to explore the world of this incredible actor.
